headlineSummary: OpenAI drew more negative sentiment on trust and PR concerns, while Anthropic skewed positive on model quality and workflow usefulness despite a critical counterpoint [E7][E8][E1][E2][E5][E6].

ranking.summary: OpenAI leaned negative, with comments questioning its media deal and describing its leadership as a cynical growth machine; the evidence mix is still limited [E7][E8].
Anthropic skewed positive, with praise for Claude Code and model quality, though one comment preferred Cursor over Claude Code for a more hands-on coding workflow [E1][E2][E5][E6].

evidence.excerpt: The benchmarks provided are for Opus-4.5, not for the latest Opus-4.6 and Qwen is still lagging in a lot of them.

annotations.rationale: Says Qwen still trails newer Opus benchmarks, which favors Anthropic's model quality in the comparison.

evidence.excerpt: I've been running Claude Code in my Cursor IDE for a while now via extension. I like the setup, and I direct Claude on one task at a time

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says they like using Claude Code in this workflow.

evidence.excerpt: It is not as intelligent as OpenAI's or Anthropic's flagship models, but I feel it has as good as or better intuition.

annotations.rationale: This gives OpenAI's flagship models credit for higher intelligence, but implies they may be weaker on intuition than the compared model.
This gives Anthropic's flagship models credit for higher intelligence, but implies they may be weaker on intuition than the compared model.

evidence.excerpt: So it has converged to the same UI/UX as the Claude/Codex desktop apps. If that's the case, why use Cursor over those more canonical apps?

annotations.rationale: Calling Claude's app one of the 'more canonical apps' is mild praise in a comparison with Cursor.
Calling Codex's app one of the 'more canonical apps' is mild praise in a comparison with Cursor.

evidence.excerpt: I realized I could just use claude code + the free version of cursor for autocomplete and it worked even better.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says using Claude Code 'worked even better,' indicating a favorable evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: It's why I use Cursor over Claude Code, I still want to _code_ not just vibe my way through tickets.

annotations.rationale: The commenter prefers Cursor over Claude Code and criticizes the Claude Code style as too much 'vibe' rather than coding.

evidence.excerpt: I bet OpenAI genuinely believes they're using their money to help free media exist. And TBPN genuinely believes this is the right choice for economic freedom so they can continue to operate. I bet they even had a convo such as "we'll never tell you what to say," and both sides genuinely believed it. But this never ends well.

annotations.rationale: The commenter argues OpenAI-backed media independence will not end well, expressing distrust of the arrangement.

evidence.excerpt: I smell a PR push to be seen as the 'good guys'. I don't buy it. The leaked emails and actions of OpenAI's leadership point to a cynical growth machine.

annotations.rationale: This directly accuses OpenAI leadership of cynical, image-driven behavior and rejects the company's 'good guys' framing.
